description: High-angle photograph offering an aerial view of a large port complex, featuring extensive land-based infrastructure for vehicle processing and multiple ferry berths. The port is situated on a coast with the sea visible in the background under a clear sky, featuring two breakwaters. Several large passenger and vehicle ferries are docked, including vessels identified as P&O Ferries and DFDS Seaways. The land area consists of vast paved surfaces for lorry and car queuing, marked with yellow lines and numerous parked vehicles, including articulated lorries. Complex multi-level road infrastructure, including flyovers and roundabouts, connects the port to the hinterland. Readable road signs indicate "DOVER EASTERN DOCKS", "PORT DOVER", "ONLY", "WAY OUT", "EXIT", "LORRY PARK 1 ONLY", "TRAFFIC", and "ONLY USE LEFT LANE FOR CARS ONLY". Various buildings, appearing to be port offices, customs facilities, and warehouses, are integrated into the infrastructure.
